{"id":"project-LICHEN-i9r0.1","title":"Spec: TDMA frame structure and slot assignment","description":"Define TDMA frame structure and slot assignment protocol.

## Location
Add new section to spec/02-physical-link.md or new spec/02a-tdma.md

## Frame structure

### Superframe
- Beacon slot (gateway TX)
- N data slots (node TX)
- Contention slot (new nodes, retries)

### Timing
- Slot duration: airtime of max packet + guard time
- At SF10/125kHz, 60-byte packet: ~200ms + 50ms guard = 250ms slot
- 4 slots/sec, 240 slots/min

### Beacon content
- Superframe number (for sync)
- Slot assignments (compressed bitmap or list)
- Next beacon time

## Slot assignment

### Static assignment
- Node IID hash mod N_SLOTS
- Simple, no coordination, but collisions if hash collides

### Dynamic assignment
- Gateway tracks active nodes
- Assigns slots in beacon or DIO option
- Reassigns on node join/leave

## Guard time
- Compensates for clock drift between beacons
- 50ms guard allows ~1% drift over 5-second superframe","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:20:38.433775Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T18:20:38.433775Z","labels":["spec","phy","protocol","scalability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-i9r0.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-i9r0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:20:38.584616Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"notes":"## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes: Ignore beacons, use ALOHA/CSMA (current behavior)
- New nodes: Sync to beacon, use assigned slots
- Mixed network: Contention slot reserved for old nodes and new-node retries

**Requirements:**
- Beacon MUST be distinguishable from regular traffic (frame type)
- Old nodes MUST ignore unknown frame types
- Contention slot MUST exist in every superframe for ALOHA traffic
- New nodes MUST accept traffic from old nodes at any time (not just slots)
- Gateway MUST receive during all slots AND contention period

**Frame structure:**
- Beacon slot: gateway TX (old nodes see it as noise, ignore)
- Assigned slots: new nodes only
- Contention slot(s): old nodes + new node fallback

**Degradation:**
- More old nodes = more contention slot usage = less TDMA benefit
- But never worse than pure ALOHA (current state)"}

new_value:
{"acceptance_criteria":"Normative spec defines GNSS-PPS clock/ASN, exact capability and control encodings, root and origin signature transcripts, dynamic join/lease/replay behavior, slot execution and guard equations, mixed-version fallback, regulatory enforcement, and objective simulator gates. Production implementation remains blocked until canonical vectors and all CCP-16 simulator gates pass.","design":"TDMA is CCP v1 scheduled mode in spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md. Every scheduled participant requires GNSS with hardware PPS; NMEA-only nodes remain on baseline CH0 CAD/CSMA. The accepted DODAG root authorizes recipient-paged leased (slot, channel, transmitter, receiver) cells. Schedules use GPS system time and ASN, root Schnorr48 authorization, dynamic assignment, one transmitter per channel per slot, no same-channel spatial reuse, CAD in every cell, bounded holdover, and immediate CH0 fallback on invalid time or lease expiry. Guard and slot duration derive from signed uncertainty/setup/occupied-time bounds rather than fixed milliseconds.","notes":"## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes: Ignore beacons, use ALOHA/CSMA (current behavior)
- New nodes: Sync to beacon, use assigned slots
- Mixed network: Contention slot reserved for old nodes and new-node retries

**Requirements:**
- Beacon MUST be distinguishable from regular traffic (frame type)
- Old nodes MUST ignore unknown frame types
- Contention slot MUST exist in every superframe for ALOHA traffic
- New nodes MUST accept traffic from old nodes at any time (not just slots)
- Gateway MUST receive during all slots AND contention period

**Frame structure:**
- Beacon slot: gateway TX (old nodes see it as noise, ignore)
- Assigned slots: new nodes only
- Contention slot(s): old nodes + new node fallback

**Degradation:**
- More old nodes = more contention slot usage = less TDMA benefit
- But never worse than pure ALOHA (current state)
Supersedes the issue sketch: no fixed 50 ms guard, no static IID-hash dedicated slots, no gateway requirement, no claim of universal zero collisions, and no assumption that legacy nodes respect contention windows.","spec_id":"spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md"}

Specified optional GNSS-PPS TDMA as CCP v1 in spec/02a-coordinated-capacity.md, including exact wire/security/timing/fallback rules and simulator gates. Documentation consistency updates accompany the profile; git diff --check passes and independent blocker reviews converged.
